children.value: This example demonstrates an external aerodynamics analysis of an aircraft wing section — computing lift and drag, visualizing the pressure coefficient distribution, and examining flow patterns.
Generate a mesh around a wing geometry
Run a RANS simulation at a given angle of attack
Compute lift and drag coefficients (Cl, Cd)
Visualize pressure coefficient (Cp) distribution on the wing surface
This halves the domain and cell count
Enclosing the leading edge, extending 0.1× chord upstream
Capture the stagnation point and leading edge suction peak
Behind the trailing edge, extending 2× chord downstream
Resolve the wake and any trailing edge separation

For wing analysis, a resolved boundary layer (y+ ≈ 1) provides more accurate lift and drag predictions than wall functions (y+ ≈ 30). Use more boundary layers (12–15) with a smaller first layer height.
Step 6: Generate Mesh and Create CFD Project

children.label: Step 6: Generate Mesh and Create CFD Project

children.value: Generate the mesh (expect 5–20 million cells for a medium-resolution wing)
Set to achieve desired Reynolds number. For Re = 6M at 1 m chord: ~88 m/s
Set via velocity direction components (e.g., Ux = V cos(α), Uz = V sin(α))
To simulate at α = 5° with freestream velocity V = 88 m/s:
Set these as the inlet velocity components.
Reference area: Wing planform area (chord × span)
near the leading edge on the upper surface (low Cp)
on the lower surface near the leading edge (Cp ≈ 1)
at mid-span, colored by velocity → Shows the flow acceleration over the upper surface and the wake
seeded upstream → Shows how flow divides at the stagnation point and flows over/under the wing
of low pressure near the wing tip → Reveals the tip vortex core
at different span stations to compare:
Cp distribution at root, mid-span, and tip
How the lift distribution varies along the span
Where stall initiates (typically from the tip inward)
To generate a lift curve (Cl vs. α):
Record Cl and Cd from each run
Plot Cl vs. α — the slope should be approximately 2π per radian in the linear region
Identify the stall angle where Cl drops sharply
RANS models (especially k-ω SST) predict stall onset reasonably well but tend to over-predict the maximum Cl. For accurate post-stall behavior, consider LES or DES (available on Pro tier and above).
